concerns: The plan should verify that the toast `left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%)` combined with the proposed `max-width: calc(100vw - 32px)` actually prevents overflow — the centering transform means the toast could still visually clip at 320px if the computed width exceeds the viewport. Consider also adding `left: 16px; right: 16px; transform: none` at ≤480px instead of relying on max-width with the centering hack.

Consolidating the existing 600px media query out of generated-site.css into responsive.css in the same PR mixes refactoring with new functionality. If responsive.css import order is wrong or something breaks, debugging is harder. Consider keeping the existing 600px block in place and only adding new rules in responsive.css, or at minimum make the consolidation a separate, clearly identified commit.

The plan doesn't address the toast's `bottom: 160px` positioning, which was calibrated for the original button stacking (24px / 80px / 136px). When button positions change at ≤480px (16px / 64px / 112px), the toast `bottom` value may also need adjustment to maintain visual consistency and avoid overlap with the top button.
